sections.heading: Overview
sections.content: Localization (l10n) adapts your app for different languages and regions; accessibility (a11y) ensures usability for users with disabilities. Android supports string translation, RTL layouts, plurals, date/number formatting, and TalkBack. Localizing expands reach globally; accessibility is ethically and legally important, improving Google Play discoverability.

rows.definition: Design app to support multiple locales without code changes
Translate strings and provide locale-specific resources
Right-to-left text direction for Arabic, Hebrew, etc.; layouts mirror automatically

sections.heading: Best Practices
items: Externalize all strings in res/values/strings.xml; use placeholders and <plurals>
Use start/end attributes and android:supportsRtl="true" for RTL
Format dates, numbers, and currency with locale-aware APIs
Provide meaningful contentDescription for all non-text elements
Ensure touch targets ≥48x48dp and sufficient color contrast (WCAG AA)
Test with TalkBack, Accessibility Scanner, Layout Inspector, and Espresso Accessibility Checks

sections.heading: Per-App Language (API 33+)
items: Set language programmatically via LocaleManager or AppCompatDelegate
System recreates app automatically on language change

sections.heading: Compose Accessibility
items: Mark decorative elements invisible with invisibleToUser()
Set role and stateDescription for controls
Use liveRegion for dynamic content announcements

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: Localization and accessibility ensure inclusive apps. Externalize strings, handle plurals, support RTL, format locale-aware content, provide content descriptions, test touch targets and contrast, and leverage Compose semantics. These practices expand your audience and improve user experience globally.
